column1: #Summary1=(human1) After being grounded for staying out at night, Kendra fights with her mother, who does not permit her to join the volleyball team and later talks to her partner about cleaning the house's insulation to prevent mice from spreading an ongoing Hantavirus epidemic.

#Summary2=(human2) Kendra, who is grounded for allegedly staying out last night, and others have a conversation with her mom about what happened last night and whether Kendra’s friends are lying to cover for her, followed by her mom not allowing her to join the volleyball team and a mention of hantavirus and cleaning up insulation with dead mice in it.

#Summary3=(human3) Kendra, Sabrina, and their mom argue about whether it was fair for Kendra to be grounded for spending the night at a friend's house (an act Kendra denies), and then the mom and another person talk about removing insulation contaminated with mouse feces.

#Summary4=(human4) In a tense family conversation, Kendra attempts to convince her mom that she did not spend the night at her friend’s house and of the unfairness of being grounded for the misunderstood situation.

#Summary5=(human5) Kendra bemoans that she has been wrongfully grounded because her mother thinks she spent the night at a friend's place, so Kendra tries convincing her mom that she came home last night, but her mom doesn't believe her as she has lost trust in Kendra.
#Text=I left the phone in my bedroom last night , and so I 'm grounded from the phone .
#Text=You 're so stupid thinking I spent the night .
#Text=I came home last night and told you .
#Text=She thinks I spent the night in my jeans —
#Text=If I did spend the night , and I was trying to lie , I would give up , cause then I would n't care , cause I knew I deserved it .
#Text=But I did n't spend the night , and I do n't deserve this .
#Text=Kim could n't spend the night , I told you .
#Text=She could , but then af- — I wanted to stay at the game longer .
#Text=And her mom wanted to take her home early , and I 'm like , no let 's stay longer .
#Text=But her mom would n't let her .
#Text=And so she went home and , I was like , I 'll call you when I get home , and then you come over .
#Text=And she goes , no just spend the night .
#Text=Okay cool , cause she had to go bowling in the morning .
#Text=Right , Melanie will call me to confirm your lie .
#Text=You 'll get a hold of her first .
#Text=I thought Melanie did n't have a phone .
#Text=But I 'm supposed to call Melanie 's mom .
#Text=I did n't want you to have her phone number .
#Text=A- and I 'm supposed to trust you every time you tell the truth .
#Text=But how am I supposed to know when you 're telling the truth ?
#Text=Yeah , and I 'm supposed to read your mind and know what you 're joking about .
#Text=You know Kendra , y- — I do n't know how many times I got ta tell you , once you lie , once you lie —
#Text=You 're not going anywhere for a while .
#Text=God , I did n't spend the night , that 's what makes me so mad , I 'm grounded for nothing .
#Text=I was home all night last night , I came home and told you .
#Text=And even my cuffs and everything 's on my bed .
#Text=Next time if I leave in the morning , I 'll wake you up .
#Text=Well you woke me up last night , to tell me Kim was n't spending the night .
#Text=I do n't know why you could n't wake me up in the morning , to tell me that you were going .
#Text=You guys wake me up every time I 'm trying to take a little nap .
#Text=I left it in a ponytail , I did n't even have time to take a shower .
#Text=I think if I spent the night I would 've took a shower .
#Text=All I need is your signature so I can play the volleyball .
#Text=It 's f- like on Saturdays and Wednesdays .
#Text=Marlena did it a long time ago , but it has n't been watered .
#Text=See if I spent the night mom , you 'd think I 'd still be in my uniform ?
#Text=See now she 's trying to think of ways to cover her tracks .
#Text=Cause I wanted to go to the bathroom when you 're done .
#Text=Do you remember the uh , program on TV , about Hantavirus ?
#Text=I do n't re- – I do n't remember .
#Text=It 's the one from the dead mice .
#Text=Is it , is it all over now ?
#Text=Is it in the United States now ?
#Text=They said , they already determined it was in like eighteen different states .
#Text=That insulation out there with the mice shit in it ?
#Text=I mean that 's the thing I thought of too .
#Text=Well , it 's all packed up in garbage bags now .
#Text=Find something else to pick it up with .
#Text=Did you — did you put it in a trash bag ?
#Text=I took that brown trash thing , and tucked a bag in it .
#Text=And then I put them in there .
